reviews: Simple Breakfast. Bee hoon + noodles + vegetables + luncheon meat + five spice roll (ngoh hiang) = S$3.60.

econ bee hoon/holland. This corner stall has been seeing long queues and loyal customers over the years. This corner stall always has people lining up to buy their fried bee hoon/mee and choosing a plethora of ingredients to go with it.
Economic Fried Bee Hoon | $3.00. Fried bee hoon with 3 fried eggs, cabbage and cuttlefish fillet.
Economic Fried Bee Hoon | $3.80. Stir fry vegetables, 3x fried eggs and 2x sotong fillet on top of white bee hoon.
Economic Fried Bee Hoon | $4.30. Stir fry vegetables, 2x fried eggs, 1x luncheon meat and 1x siting fillet on top of white bee hoon.
Economic Fried Bee Hoon | $2. Had fish fillet, stir fry cabbage, egg. Slightly moist bee hoon which I liked.

reviews: Nasi Lemak | $5.00. The rice was so fragrant with coconut. It was soft and fluffy and I was able to taste the coconut milk...
Premium Laksa [~$4.50]. The laksa gravy was slightly diluted but the prawns was on point. Fresh and crunchy...
Nasi Lemak | $5.80. Chicken thigh set with additional fish fillet. Set comes with chicken thigh, vegetables, fried egg, peanuts and ikan bilis.
Fantastic and Affordable Nasi Lemak. Famous Nasi Lemak from Holland Village. For an affordable price of $4-$6, it offers high value.
Good Enough Nasi Lemak. This nasi lemak stall has been here since the beginning of times and the standards have remained pretty consistent.
363 Holland Village Nasi Lemak. Weekend breakfasts are made of these. Oozy egg atop fluffy, fragrant coconut rice.
